title: rubus hirtus var. angulatus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. angulatus, also known as the Himalayan Raspberry or Himalayan Bramble, is a plant species in the Rubus genus, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. angulatus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as jams and preserves, which can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its medicinal and nutritional benefits.
